behavior_in_task_description.reason: All file paths, JSON keys (hookSpecificOutput, hookEventName, additionalContext), required fields (name, version, description, author), and required content keywords (explanatory, insight, educational, backticks) are explicitly named in instruction.md.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes structure and required keys/content but doesn't provide the exact script contents; agent must still write the logic.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check structural requirements (file existence, valid JSON, required keys, keyword presence) that any correct implementation would satisfy, not gold-specific strings.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests execute the shell script via subprocess.run and parse JSON output, not just text grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require actual file creation, valid JSON output with specific keys, and executable script — a stub couldn't pass.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All described behaviors (plugin files, manifest fields, hooks config, script executable, keyword content) are tested.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comment headers (fail-to-pass, pass-to-pass static, repo_tests) and descriptive docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: JSON schemas for plugin.json and hooks.json are documented with required fields and example structure in instruction.md.

no_typos.reason: Paths and names are consistent between instruction.md, tests, and solution.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Paths are part of the task requirement (creating new plugin), not leakage of a bug location.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: This is a new plugin creation task, not a config edit task.
